titre: Technicien (ne) en informatique en support technique
description: Nous sommes à la recherche de personnel en informatique au niveau du support technique pour faire différents contrats dans différents ministères de la fonction publique fédérale ( Gouvernement du Canada). Les tâches typiques peuvent comprendre ce qui suit : - Coordonner l'élaboration des spécifications d'applications technologiques émergentes. - Agir comme agent de liaison pour le soutien des utilisateurs entre les fournisseurs, les techniciens des systèmes d'information et les ministères des utilisateurs finaux. - Partager son savoir-faire et fournir des directives dans le domaine de la formation sur le soutien pour les nouvelles technologies et les applications spéciales. - Simuler ou reproduire les problèmes techniques rencontrés les utilisateurs. - Fournir des conseils et une formation aux utilisateurs en réponse aux problèmes rencontrés. - Recommander et mettre en oeuvre des exigences complexes en matière de sécurité. - Fournir du soutien pour les systèmes administratifs, les réseaux et Internet aux utilisateurs en réponse aux problèmes rencontrés. - Consigner, organiser et tenir à jour les problèmes et les solutions dans un registre qui sera utilisé par des collègues. - Vérifier l'exactitude et l'exhaustivité du travail accompli. - Concevoir, configurer et déployer des systèmes de fichiers et des applications sur serveur. - Identifier, rechercher et résoudre des problèmes techniques complexes. - Recommander de nouveaux produits ou processus qui prennent en charge et améliorent les activités commerciales des groupes d'utilisateurs. - Planifier la mise en oeuvre d'une mise à niveau du système à l'échelle du ministère ou participer à sa mise en oeuvre. Exigences: Études : - Certificat ou diplôme en science informatique ou dans un autre domaine pertinent décerné par un établissement d'enseignement postsecondaire canadien reconnu. - Expérience : 24 mois d'expérience pertinente. - Avoir une cote de sécurité du Gouvernement du Canada de niveau Secret ou être disposé à effectuer le processus pour l'obtenir. Salaire : Le salaire est variable selon les contrats pour les différents ministères.

Votre mission

 NOZ, % bonnes affaires – % économie circulaire – Recrute Technicien Support niveau 1 (H/F) en CDI


 Envie de rejoindre une entreprise en plein développement ?


 L’Univers NOZ, leader européen du déstockage, compte 6 collaborateurs dans le monde et plus de magasins en France. En croissance continue depuis 49 ans, le groupe connaît aujourd’hui une forte expansion avec l’ouverture de nombreux magasins et l’ambition de devenir le leader mondial du déstockage.


 Dans ce contexte dynamique, nous recrutons un Technicien Support Niveau 1 (H/F) en CDI pour renforcer notre équipe au sein du service Informatique.


 Objectif du poste : Assurer la mise en service, le bon fonctionnement et la continuité des équipements informatiques et téléphoniques des postes de travail, tout en garantissant un support efficace aux utilisateurs dans le respect des procédures et des normes de sécurité.

 Missions


 Vos missions, si vous les acceptez, s’articuleront autour des axes suivants :


 Installation

 
 Réaliser l’installation et la configuration des équipements informatiques et téléphoniques (postes de travail, applications, matériels de téléphonie et périphériques)


 
 
 Déployer les mises à jour logicielles et systèmes


 Exploitation

 
 Prendre en charge et traiter les incidents à distance (postes, réseaux, messagerie, téléphonie)


 
 
 Diagnostiquer les dysfonctionnements et assurer leur résolution


 
 
 Gérer les incidents en coordination avec les équipes support si nécessaire


 
 
 Assurer la gestion et le suivi du parc informatique connecté au réseau


 Maintenance, administration et sécurité

 
 Administrer la messagerie côté poste de travail (configuration, accès, utilisation)


 
 
 Mettre en œuvre les outils de télédistribution et de télémaintenance (profils, cibles, dépendances)


 
 
 Veiller à la conformité des équipements avec les référentiels internes


 Support

 
 Prendre en charge les demandes des utilisateurs (à distance et en proximité)


 
 
 Accueillir et accompagner les utilisateurs au support


 
 
 Assurer une assistance technique sur les équipements et logiciels


 
 
 Former et accompagner les utilisateurs dans la prise en main des outils


 
 
 Enregistrer et suivre les incidents via l’outil de ticketing (GLPI)


 
 
 Garantir la qualité et les délais de traitement des demandes de matériel et logiciels


 Condition particulière : Astreintes à prévoir en semaine et le week-end (environ 1 week-end sur 3)


 

Profil recherché

Profil recherché :

Formation et expérience

-Bac +2 en informatique

-Première expérience sur un poste similaire (alternance acceptée)

Compétences techniques

-Maîtrise de l’environnement Windows (Windows 11, Windows Server)

-Connaissances en réseaux (DHC, DNS, VLAN…)

-Maîtrise d’Active Directory et des outils de messagerie (Exchange)

-Capacité à diagnostiquer et résoudre des incidents à distance

Qualités personnelles

-Rigueur, sens de l’organisation et autonomie

-Bon relationnel, aisance téléphonique et sens du service

-Capacité d’écoute et de communication

-Réactivité et esprit d’analyse

-Discrétion et respect de la confidentialité (déontologie informatique)

Ce que Noz vous offre :

-Un rôle central dans une entreprise en forte croissance.

-La possibilité d’impacter directement la dynamique humaine de votre périmètre.

-Un environnement stimulant et proche des opérationnels.

Sur place, profitez de nombreux services et avantages :

-Services pratiques : réception de colis, location de vélos électriques, etc.

-Vie de campus : crèche avec mini-ferme, cours de sport, potager partagé.

-Ambiance : un environnement convivial au sein d’une entreprise à taille humaine… mais à portée internationale 

NOZ s’engage pour l’inclusion : à compétences égales, toutes les candidatures sont étudiées, y compris celles de personnes en situation de handicap.

IT Systems & Network Engineer (Infrastructure Support)


If you're the person who gets pulled in when systems slow down, Wi-Fi drops, or users can't access what they need — this is your lane.


This role sits right between end-user support, systems admin, and network operations. You're not siloed. You touch everything.


Role Details
Location: Vancouver, BC, Canada


Work Model: Onsite (based on customer needs)


Type: Permanent / Direct Hire


You'll be the go-to for day-to-day stability across systems, networks, and user environments.


This includes
Resolving real-time issues


Keeping infrastructure healthy
Fixing recurring problems at the root (not patching symptoms)


Environment
Ops-heavy, support-driven environment


Mix of on-prem + cloud (M365 / Azure / AWS)


Direct interaction with users, vendors, and internal teams


Structured change control and operational processes


What this role actually owns day-to-day


Handling incidents across devices, servers, and network connectivity


Managing AD, DNS, DHCP, and core services


Supporting Wi-Fi, endpoint issues, and access problems


Running patching, backups, monitoring, and maintenance


Working tickets end-to-end — not passing them around


Key Responsibilities
Troubleshoot system, network, and application access issues across users and infrastructure


Support Windows/Linux servers + core services (AD, DNS, DHCP, file/print)


Resolve Wi-Fi and connectivity issues across environments


Monitor system health, respond to alerts, and stabilize performance


Perform root cause analysis on recurring issues and implement permanent fixes


Execute changes to infrastructure with proper validation and rollback planning


Work with vendors (ISP, cloud, hardware) to resolve escalations


Maintain documentation, runbooks, and knowledge base articles


Deploy and configure endpoints (laptops, PCs, peripherals)


Support cloud workloads across Microsoft 365, Azure, or AWS


Must-Have Requirements


Hands-on experience with Windows or Linux server administration


Strong troubleshooting across network + systems + end-user issues


Experience with AD, DNS, DHCP


Exposure to cloud platforms (M365 / Azure / AWS)


Proven ability to handle incident resolution in live environments


Experience working in support-driven or operations environments

titre: Senior Project Manager - Community Infrastructure (First Nations & Municipal)
description:
Job Description

What if you could redefine what’s possible? With us, you can.

You want Purpose. Growth. Opportunity. People who get it.

We are the home of ambitious, passionate, and innovative world shapers.

With an unmatched breadth and depth of engineering, advisory and science-based expertise, our global minds unite to power local solutions. We are pathfinders and impact makers.

We are Visioneers. We are WSP.

The Opportunity

Ready to Make an Impact? This is your chance to lead transformative projects that strengthen communities and advance sustainability. As a Senior Project Manager within our Northern Alberta & Indigenous team, you’ll guide municipal water and wastewater infrastructure projects in partnership with Indigenous communities—projects that matter for generations to come.

Applicants not residing in Edmonton will be considered.

Your Impact

Lead planning, design, and delivery of municipal water and wastewater projects for Indigenous clients, including feasibility studies, system assessments, upgrades, and new infrastructure.Build and maintain strong, respectful relationships with Indigenous communities, leadership, and project partners.Facilitate engagement sessions and workshops to ensure decisions reflect community priorities.Manage scope, budget, schedule, risk, and quality from initiation through completion.Coordinate multidisciplinary teams—engineers, planners, environmental specialists, and construction professionals.Prepare proposals and project documentation with culturally appropriate communication.Support business development and contribute to long-term Indigenous partnership strategies.Mentor junior staff and foster team growth.


The Skills That Set You Apart

10+ years managing municipal infrastructure projects, with at least 5 years working with First Nations communities.Undergraduate degree in civil engineering or civil engineering technology diploma; P.Eng., CET, or P.Tech (Eng.) designation in Alberta.PMP certification is an asset.Experience with lift stations, reservoirs, and pump stations is an advantage.Ability to present technical information in accessible, community-friendly ways.Familiarity with Indigenous Services Canada processes and willingness to travel as required.Valid Class 5 Driver’s Licence.


Why Choose WSP?

We exist to shape communities to advance humanity. The brightest engineers, advisors and scientists from across the globe call WSP home.

Proudly Canadian – we are a Top 100 Employer in Canada for 2026A global community of brilliant minds – your next idea, mentor, or opportunity is always within reach.Limitless opportunities start here. Whether it’s across the country or around the globe, we’ll help you tailor your role to match your ambition—because your growth drives ours.Flexible work, real balance – we recognise the importance of balance in our lives and encourage you to prioritise the balance in yours. #WeAreWSP


Compensation

BC, AB, SK, NWT & NT: $104,100 – $138,000

MB & ON: $95,000 – $130,800

Atlantic Provinces & QC: $94,100 – $124,900

Disclosure:

The final salary awarded for this role may vary from the above range based on several factors including, but not limited to, relevant education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, seniority, geographic location, performance, and business or organisational needs. The wage range provided in this job posting may be subject to change for business purposes.

About Us

At WSP, we exist to shape communities to advance humanity.

It’s why we bring people together who are wired to solve complex challenges - engineers, scientists, advisors, and technical experts who are driven by a common passion - to redefine what’s possible and shape what comes next. We are Visioneers.

As one of the world’s leading professional services firms, we partner with clients across transportation, infrastructure, environment, buildings, energy, water, mining and metals. We pair deep local knowledge with global insights to pioneer solutions designed to leave a lasting, positive impact.

At WSP, you’ll join a community of brilliant minds that push boundaries every day - applying deep expertise and fuelling innovation to help create a more connected and resilient future. Here, your ideas are valued, your growth is supported, and you are encouraged to bring your authentic self to work. We empower our people to collaborate across disciplines, challenge the status quo, and shape a career with purpose.

Join us.

Please Note:

Health and Safety is a core paramount value of WSP. Given the importance of keeping one another safe it is expected that you comply with our Health, Safety & Environment (HSE) policy at all times as well as client HSE policies when working at client locations. Full details here .

Some safety-sensitive positions involve fieldwork and may include work in a variety of environmental conditions, such as remote or isolated areas, working alone, and in inclement weather (within safe and reasonable limits).

WSP welcomes and encourages applications from people with disabilities. Accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process.

WSP is committed to the principles of employment equity. Only the candidates selected will be contacted.

WSP does not accept unsolicited resumes from agencies. Full details here .

Co-op May 2026 - Engineering Infrastructure Developer - 8-12-Months


Join to apply for the Co-op May 2026 - Engineering Infrastructure Developer - 8-12-Months role at General Dynamics Mission Systems–Canada. This range is provided by General Dynamics Mission Systems–Canada. Your actual pay will be based on your skills and experience — talk with your recruiter to learn more. Base pay range


CA$23.08/hr - CA$32.41/hr At General Dynamics Mission Systems–Canada, we develop innovative products and solutions that make platforms smarter — enhancing security, safety, and operational advantage while protecting nations and strengthening communities. Here, your work matters. Every day, you’ll contribute to projects that strengthen national security and advance technological capabilities.


Job Description We are looking for a co-op student to join our dynamic team where every day brings challenges and opportunities to learn new skills and make a positive impact. You will work in a fun, fast-paced, and collaborative environment helping to develop and deploy infrastructure and engineering applications to our user community. You will work alongside experienced professionals eager to share their knowledge and help you succeed.


We have a focus on deploying well-designed solutions that are maintainable, secure, and well-documented, with an emphasis on automation and DevOps best practices. Your Day to Day Will Include Building and maintaining virtual environments to host our solutions and support the team Deployment of applications and services, including integrating them together Updating and expanding applications to provide users with new features or improved processes Writing scripts or tools, automating tasks such as setup, provisioning, environment monitoring, data collection, and reporting Evaluating new tools and technologies to improve the way we work Developing, streamlining, and communicating processes and best practices Qualifications


Essential Qualifications Currently pursuing a Bachelor’s degree or College Diploma in Engineering or Computer Science Experience working with scripting languages such as Python, Bash, or PowerShell Experience working with JavaScript or TypeScript Fundamental understanding of Server and Desktop Operating systems Love working in a team environment, collaborating, and sharing your knowledge Ability to analyze complex issues, develop and recommend options, and implement solutions Good communication skills, both written and verbal Team Impact You will bring to our team your enthusiasm to contribute, a willingness to learn new skills and tools, and your strong sense of ownership, as well as your disciplined approach to solution development. You won’t be afraid to ask “why are we doing things this way?” and “how can we make it better?” Nice to Have Experience working in a git environment with a modern IDE or directly on the command-line Experience working with C#, ASP.NET, and Angular. Practice working with Linux and/or Windows operating systems Any knowledge of VM environments or containerization tools like Docker It is a requirement that General Dynamics Mission Systems–Canada be registered with the Canadian Controlled Goods program and that all of its workforce be security assessed.


Successful applicants must meet all applicable security requirements, including but not limited to the ability to obtain and maintain a Canadian government security clearance. Applicants may be required to meet additional security requirements in order to gain access to technical data, classified areas or information that is subject to international regulations. You must be eligible to work in Canada.


Additional
Information
Compensation The expected hourly rate for this position is CA$23.08 – CA$32.41. Final compensation will be determined based on your relevant work experience to date. As part of student placement, you will be eligible for quarterly pay increases.


Why Students Love Working Here Purposeful


Innovation & Problem Solving: You’ll work alongside experienced professionals and some of the brightest minds in the defence sector, gaining hands-on experience with next-generation technologies. Connection & Mentorship: You will work with teammates that are excited to share their experience and develop your skills. Perks & Highlights: Eligibility for future co-ops or full-time roles Flexible work hours and compressed workweek options Hybrid work model (where applicable) Networking opportunities across teams and disciplines Onsite amenities and social events Corporate discounts Accommodation


If you require accommodation during any stage of the application process, please contact Human Resources via recruitment@gd-ms.ca.

titre: Executive Leader for Major Civil Infrastructure Projects in North America
description:
Drive large-scale heavy civil Public-Private Partnership projects as an Executive Leader. Oversee field operations, contractor performance, and risk management while ensuring project delivery and client satisfaction across multiple locations. As the Vice President of Civil Asset Delivery, you will provide executive oversight on complex infrastructure projects, ensuring they meet budget, schedule, and safety standards.


Your role will involve managing a diverse portfolio, leading project teams, and resolving operational challenges while maintaining strong relationships with stakeholders. This hands-on position requires experience in heavy civil construction and an ability to navigate multi-project environments. Key Responsibilities:


Oversee active major infrastructure projects and monitor performanceChampion safety culture and operational discipline on project sitesLead resolution of construction risks and operational challengesManage project governance, reporting, and performance oversightSupport construction strategy for major infrastructure pursuits Requirements:12+ years in heavy civil infrastructure or major constructionMinimum 2 years in an executive role within a PPP/P3 organizationProven experience with large infrastructure projects (roads, bridges)Strong stakeholder management and team leadership capabilitiesHands-on approach to project engagement and problem-solving Utilize your extensive experience to drive large-scale projects and lead teams in delivering exceptional infrastructure solutions.

The AVP, DevOps and Release will be working closely with Quality Assurance, Development, Project Management and Database teams in creating builds/executables, building smoke tests, deploying new builds to test and production environments, automating builds and deployments, creating/maintaining thorough deployment and environment documentation and troubleshooting release related issues.


MAJOR RESPONSIBILITIES

Manage a team of Release engineers and DBAs, and a tech currency team.
Accountable for the infrastructure and deployment needs of the project teams and support teams.
Accountable for the infrastructure and applications (on-prem and Azure), and their on-going support.
Accountable for the currency/upgrades of these environments (infrastructure upgrades).
Maintain and enhance the process documentation around noted activities.
Maintain documentation of non-production and production environments.
Work closely with stakeholders to prioritize requests and set expectations.
Work closely with Development teams to maintain existing applications and onboard new applications.
Accountable for the security of these environments including adherence to Chubb Global security and infrastructure requirements and respond to developing security risks.
Implement and maintain DevSecOps (CI/CD) pipelines for applications.
Migrate applications from the on-prem data center to Azure.
Manage monthly and off cycle production deployments.
Other activities as assigned

QUALIFICATIONS

Completed Bachelor’s Degree (software engineering, computer programming) preferred.
Strong knowledge of infrastructure and networking is required.
Strong knowledge of scripting is required.
Strong knowledge of build and deployments tools such Ant and Maven is required.
Knowledge of containers and building CI/CD pipelines is required.
Experience with Java/JEE, Websphere, Spring, .net, SQL Server will be an asset.
Knowledge of cloud will be an asset.
5+ years experience in a leadership role managing IT teams.
Experience with leveraging AI to improve productivity.

Must be available to work onsite in Ottawa. Only candidates who hold an active Secret Clearance will be considered. Being bilingual is an asset. Must have 5+ years of Help Desk experience.


Job Description
Successful candidates will be providing various levels of IM/IT services to clients via telephone, Information Technology Service Management (ITSM) tools, and/or email. The Help Desk Specialist will resolve and/or instruct clients on IM/IT issues and/or answer their IT-related queries.


Provide on-site problem determination and resolution in direct support of the following: Personal Computers (PCs), laptops, tablets, monitors, equipment, printers, scanners, rollout activities, software/hardware installation, and troubleshooting including Microsoft Office & Outlook within an Exchange environment, client moves, and Active Directory administration in accordance with the Standard Operating Procedures.


Seniority levelMid-Senior levelEmployment typeContractJob functionInformation TechnologyIndustriesIT Services and IT Consulting

V3 Companies, a multidisciplinary AEC firm, is seeking a Help Desk Technician in our Sherwood Park office, where you will support a passionate team with your exceptional information technology skills that not only help get the job done, but keep progress in motion.


 At V3, our 450+ designers, engineers, and scientists change the shape of our communities through the work we do. As part of our Information Technology team, you will help support the technical needs of our team as they make meaningful, effective change across North America.


  Here’s what you’ll do:
 
  Set up, modify, and decommission user accounts and systems, including Active Directory, Microsoft 365, CRM, and network domain access.
  Build, image, configure, and deploy laptops and desktops; create and maintain user profiles.
  Troubleshoot operating system issues and perform basic hardware diagnostics.
  Provide support and basic maintenance for printers, copiers, and plotters.
  Deliver remote IT support to users in field offices and home office environments.
  Perform backup restores and assist with routine system maintenance activities.
  Support Microsoft 365 services (Outlook, Teams, OneDrive/SharePoint), including access issues, mailbox configuration, and basic collaboration setup.
  Provide basic network connectivity support (LAN/Wi‑Fi, VPN, and remote access), escalating complex issues to senior IT staff as needed.
  Assist with endpoint security tasks such as antivirus/EDR checks, patching support, and adherence to IT security policies.
  Maintain accurate IT inventory records and support device lifecycle activities, including receiving, tagging, deployment, swaps, returns, and e‑waste processes.
  Provide IT onboarding and basic orientation for new V3 team members.
  Coordinate with vendors and internal teams as needed and communicate timely status updates to users.
  Support other IT tasks and projects as assigned.
  Participate in occasional evening or weekend work to support off-hours upgrades, deployments, or urgent support needs.
  Provide proactive help desk support by conducting initial diagnostics, troubleshooting, and resolving issues efficiently. Accurately log, track, and document all incidents, service requests, assets, troubleshooting actions, and outcomes within the ticketing system to ensure clear communication and seamless resolution.
  Occasional travel to other V3 regional offices as required for projects.
 
 We’re looking for:
 
  2–5 years of related IT experience (preferred).
  Working knowledge of Windows operating systems, basic hardware troubleshooting, and common business applications.
  Experience with ticketing systems and clear documentation of issue details, troubleshooting steps, and resolutions.
  Familiarity with user and access administration in Active Directory and Microsoft 365, including account provisioning, password resets, and MFA support.
  Familiarity of networking concepts such as IP addressing, DNS/DHCP, Wi‑Fi, and VPN/remote access (an asset).
  Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to prioritize work in a fast-paced environment.
  Customer-service mindset with the ability to communicate technical concepts clearly to non-technical users.
  Strong time management, collaboration, and verbal communication skills.
  Ability to create and maintain basic documentation and knowledge-based articles.
 
 We offer:
 
  Competitive salary, benefits, and performance-based bonuses
  Retirement plan with up to 6% company RRSP match
  Paid time off, holidays, and volunteer paid time off
  Professional development opportunities
  Collaborative and supportive work environment
 
 Why V3?
 We empower our people with the skills and spirit to achieve their goals and make impactful transformations in the places we live and work. Our growing firm has offices in Alberta, the Carolinas, Colorado, Illinois, Indiana, and Ohio, allowing for expert collaboration across North America.


 Working with us opens the door to:


 
  Exciting projects that positively influence the world around you
  Goal-driven career development, training, and mentorship programs
  Cutting-edge technology with a dedicated technology advancement committee
  Employee-led enrichment programs devoted to wellness, community impact, and more
  Firm ownership opportunities
 
 Your professional aspirations are our top priority: this is your career, and we’re here to support that vision.


 With the right team, you can change the world. Join V3 and go beyond limits.


  Here are your next steps:
 To help us get acquainted, please send your resume, portfolio and a cover letter introducing yourself to: hrgroup@v3co.com.
 We look forward to learning more about you and exploring how you could contribute to our team.

titre: IT Helpdesk Manager Leading Support Teams
description:
Drive exceptional technical support as an IT Helpdesk Manager. Lead a dedicated team to ensure timely service delivery, user satisfaction, and system reliability while fostering a culture of continuous improvement.


In this managerial role, you'll oversee the daily operations of the IT Helpdesk, focused on mentoring a high-performing team. You will optimize technical support aligned with service level agreements and manage incident responses effectively. Collaboration with various IT functions is essential for seamless service delivery and the enhancement of employee experiences.

Mentor and develop the IT Help Desk teamOversee daily help desk operations and ticket managementEnsure compliance with IT policies and standardsManage incident response and post-incident reviewsCollaborate with IT functions for swift service resolution
Requirements
Bachelor’s degree with 8+ years in IT3+ years in a leadership role in ITITIL certification or ITIL familiarity requiredStrong cloud services technical experienceProven ability to build high-performance teams
Lead the IT Helpdesk with a focus on support excellence, process improvement, and operational efficiency.

Company Description
 

   Keeran Networks is a rapidly growing Managed Services Provider headquartered in Edmonton. We have an excellent team, great work environment and wonderful clients to work with. We are currently seeking a full-time Help Desk Analyst to be based in our Edmonton office.


 As a Managed Services Provider, we tend to be exposed to more technologies than most internal IT positions, and we certainly support a broader variety of network environments. The pace and demands of our environment are also much greater than an internal IT position. The successfully candidate will be a highly organized individual who is a great communicator.



   Job Description
 
  

    Provide front line support to clients from many different industries, ranging from dental offices to car dealerships
  Interact with clients remotely via telephone (80%) and in person at their locations (20%)
  Provide complete support for client environments including hardware, operating systems, line of business applications and network equipment
  Work with vendor support on behalf of our clients to ensure their support issues are resolved
  Focused on completing a higher volume of tickets, the majority of which should be possible to complete in on hour or less
  Escalate tickets to tier 2 as required, and ensure that all steps have been properly documented before escalating
  Use, update and contribute content to our internal knowledge base
  Client site visits to resolve issues within skillset
  Implementation projects as required and able, such as desktop deployments
  Maintain a calm and professional attitude even when dealing with stressful situations
  Earn the trust and respect of clients
  Works on obtaining industry certifications to further career path
  Time entry in real time
 

   Qualifications
 
  

    Education: 1-2 years of relevant study and diploma preferred, but relevant work experience can be a substitute
  Preference will be given to candidates who have previous been employed at a Managed Services Provider
  Experience with Windows desktop and server support and networking
  Strong and effective oral and written communicator
  Strong time management skills, multitasking skills, and the ability to prioritize tasks with minimal supervision
  Professional, responsive, and positive work attitude is essential
 

   Additional Information
 

   NOTE: This job description is not intended to be all-inclusive. Employee may perform other related duties as negotiated to meet the ongoing needs of the organization.

 When applying, please state the number of years of experience in your response along with your resume.

Job Type: Full Time
Hours: Vary
Salary: $22.85 CAD DOE


About AnswerNet 
AnswerNet is the brand name for the AnswerNet family of companies including AnswerNet, Inc., New AnswerNet Inc., Cerida Investment Corp., TPV.com, and Ansercomm to name a few. Together with our affiliates, AnswerNet operates more than 20 contact centers within the continental United States and Canada. We provide a vast range of services to optimize telephone answering services, appointment setting and confirmation, customer support, third-party verification, sales, lead qualification, market research, and a host of other contact management solutions. In all, AnswerNet has more than 10,000 satisfied clients and we process over 125 million interactions per year.

Summary of Position:
Customer-focused L1/L2 Helpdesk Technician responsible for providing first and second-level technical support to end-users for issues related to hardware, software, and network systems. This position will involve researching, diagnosing, troubleshooting, finding solutions, and taking ownership of customer-reported issues to ensure they are resolved. When necessary, unresolved issues should be escalated to the appropriate internal teams following standard procedures. The ideal candidate will possess excellent communication skills, a strong technical background, and the ability to efficiently troubleshoot and resolve issues.
ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
• Inbound and Outbound Calls
• Serve as the first point of contact for end-users seeking technical assistance via phone, email, or helpdesk tickets.
• Root cause analysis probing
• Providing immediate solutions to common user issues.
• Ticket Routing
• Document accurate records of all support requests, resolutions, and follow-ups.
• Knowledge Base Utilization
• Responsible for the ongoing maintenance of AnswerNet's desktop environment.
• Creates and maintains user e-mail accounts.
• Virtual Machine and or Remote Desktop support
• Oversee monitoring network systems, including network outages.
• Ensuring service level agreements are met.
• Managing user accounts and their associated attributes, such as name, email address, password, security groups, and multifactor authentication in Azure AD, or through Microsoft Active Directory on Windows Server 2016, 2019.
• Install, configure, and troubleshoot hardware, software, systems, networks, printers, and scanners.
Required Qualifications:
• Ability to work from home (this is a remote position)
• Prior Technical Support or Help Desk experience
• High School Diploma or equivalent
• Phone Etiquette
• History of Customer Service
• Excellent verbal, writing, grammar, and communication skills
• Critical Thinking and Problem-Solving skills
• Attention to Detail
• Familiarity with Help Desk Applications (Ex. Zendesk, FreshService)
• Familiarity with VOIP or Telephony services
• Ability to provide step-by-step technical help, both written and verbal
• Relevant certifications (e.g., CompTIA A+, Network+, Microsoft Certified IT Professional) are a plus
WAH Requirements:
• PC/Laptop with at least Windows 10 (Apple/Mac products, Chromebooks, and tablets are not compatible)
• Hard-wired high-speed internet connection (ethernet cable)
• USB-connected Headset
• Webcam
• A quiet dedicated place to work free from distractions including pets and children.

Chez Newforma, vous contribuerez à façonner l’avenir de la gestion de l’information de projet pour les architectes, ingénieurs et entrepreneurs du monde entier. Rejoignez une équipe à laquelle font confiance plus de 1 500 firmes pour simplifier leur façon de travailler. Ensemble, nous créons des outils qui relient les personnes à l’information dont elles ont besoin, plus rapidement et plus intelligemment. Bâtissons quelque chose d’exceptionnel.




Nous sommes à la recherche d’un(e) technicien(ne) en soutien informatique bilingue (anglais et français) junior afin de fournir un soutien technique de première ligne, et occasionnellement de deuxième ligne, pour le matériel et les logiciels, auprès des utilisateurs internes de nos différentes entités.




Dans ce rôle, vous utiliserez notre plateforme corporative de gestion de services TI (ITSM) afin de créer, mettre à jour et résoudre les billets de soutien, tout en documentant rigoureusement les problématiques signalées, les étapes de dépannage et les solutions apportées. Ce poste convient particulièrement aux candidat(e)s souhaitant développer leurs compétences techniques et en service à la clientèle dans un environnement structuré et mature.




Responsabilités principales

Fournir une assistance et un soutien techniques aux utilisateurs finaux pour les systèmes informatiques, le matériel et les logiciels.Déployer, surveiller, mettre à niveau et soutenir l’ensemble des systèmes TI globaux, y compris les serveurs, PC, Mac, imprimantes, systèmes d’exploitation, applications logicielles et périphériques.Diagnostiquer et assurer la maintenance du matériel utilisé par les utilisateurs (ordinateurs portables et de bureau, appareils, imprimantes, etc.).Répondre aux demandes, créer et mettre à jour les billets, identifier les problèmes et déterminer puis mettre en œuvre les solutions ou les procédures d’escalade.Prioriser et compléter le travail afin d’atteindre ou de dépasser les cibles de temps de réponse et de résolution.Appliquer les politiques et procédures établies pour la gestion des flux de travail, y compris l’escalade des billets.Utiliser les ressources disponibles afin d’identifier rapidement et avec précision les solutions.Maintenir l’inventaire de l’équipement, du matériel, des logiciels et des fournitures.Comprendre, appliquer et exploiter les informations acquises par la lecture et l’analyse de la documentation technique, des manuels et d’autres sources de soutien.Effectuer toute autre tâche connexe assignée.


Exigences du poste

1 à 2 ans d’expérience dans un rôle similaire.Solides compétences en résolution de problèmes et intérêt marqué pour l’amélioration continue des solutions technologiques.Bilinguisme anglais et français, à l’oral comme à l’écrit.Excellentes compétences en communication écrite et verbale, avec la capacité d’interagir avec une grande diversité d’interlocuteurs.Capacité démontrée à interagir efficacement avec les utilisateurs finaux afin d’identifier et de résoudre des problèmes techniques.Bonne compréhension des environnements serveurs Microsoft Windows sur site et des produits infonuagiques Microsoft 365, incluant Entra ID, Intune, Defender, Power Platform, SharePoint, l’accès conditionnel, etc.Connaissance fonctionnelle des VPN utilisateurs.Expérience avec VMware vSphere et vCenter.Capacité à travailler en équipe comme de façon autonome, tout en gérant plusieurs tâches simultanément.


Atouts souhaitables

Expérience en service et soutien à la clientèle.Expérience avec les réseaux VPN, Wi-Fi et autres infrastructures réseau.Expérience avec des services infonuagiques tels qu’Azure et AWS.Expérience avec Newforma Konekt, Newforma Project Center, ainsi qu’avec les produits mobiles et infonuagiques de Newforma.


Pourquoi travailler chez Newforma ?

Un travail porteur de sens : Aider les professionnel(le)s de l’industrie AECO à résoudre des enjeux concrets.Un impact mondial : Nos outils sont utilisés sur plus de 16 millions de projets à travers le monde.Une culture collaborative : Travaillez avec des collègues talentueux qui valorisent vos idées et votre contribution.Des possibilités de croissance : Nous soutenons votre développement professionnel grâce à la formation et au mentorat.L’innovation au cœur de nos activités : Faites partie d’une entreprise en constante évolution pour répondre aux besoins de l’industrie.

🌟 Join Our Team: Trilingual Help Desk Level 2 Support (Spanish + English + French) 🌍




Are you passionate about IT support and fluent in Spanish, English and French? We're looking for a Trilingual Help Desk Level 2 Specialist to provide stellar technical assistance for our global clients




Work from home and make an impact in the fast-paced world of IT support.




Key Responsibilities:

📞 User Assistance: Offer technical support via phone, chat, or email. Assist with password resets, access requests, and basic IT training.

📋 Incident Management: Log and manage incidents, ensuring they're resolved within the SLA timeframe. Keep users updated on their ticket status.

💻 Problem Solving: Troubleshoot issues related to hardware, software, and network connectivity. Apply known solutions or escalate as needed.

🚀 Escalation: Pass on complex problems to higher-level support, while ensuring users are fully informed.

💬 Customer Service: Deliver excellent customer service, ensuring a positive experience with each interaction.

🔐 Compliance & Security: Follow IT security standards and company policies, ensuring adherence to all relevant regulations.




What We’re Looking For:

🛠️ Experience: Previous experience as an L2 Help Desk specialist, plus professional studies in IT is desirable.

🌐 Technical Skills: Proficiency in troubleshooting hardware, software, and networks. Knowledge of Office 365, Windows 10-11, Active Directory, and Exchange servers.

🗣️ Languages: Advanced English (C1) and French (B2) are required. Excellent communication skills in both languages.

⏱️ Time Management: Strong organizational skills to handle multiple tasks efficiently.




📅 Position Details:

🗓️ Schedule: To be determined / Open schedule availability required.

💰 Salary: To be disclosed upon contact

📋 Contract Type: Long term contract

🌍 Location: Work from home Open to Mexico, Guatemala, Honduras, El Salvador, Colombia and Peru

💻 Equipment Provided: Yes




💥 Submit Your Resume 💥

📲 Send your up-to-date resume in English via WhatsApp at +1 (945) 888-0926

titre: Telecom Construction Worker - Direct Hire Opportunity
description:
Our client, a telecommunications company, is building the future of connectivity and they need skilled hands to do it! 


As a Construction Worker, you will play a key role in infrastructure development for fiber optic and wireless networks. If you are hardworking, safety-conscious, and ready to work in the field rain or shine, we want to hear from you!


What you get to do: 


• Preps sites for underground Telecom installations
• Manually locates buried utility lines
• Fits together the proper conduit for the job at hand
• Places and maintains cable/fiber within underground conduit system
• Organizes supplies and materials for scheduled jobs
• Loads and unloads compactor/materials
• Sets Taps and Nids on buildings
• Pull cable/fiber through existing conduit
• Leaves work area neat using rakes, brooms and shovels
• Maintains right-of-ways, including patching roadways and backfill areas
• Installs and maintains pedestals, carrier sites and equipment at subscriber locations
• Operates digging and trenching equipment
• All tasks are done outdoors
• Operate as a Team Member
• Other duties as assigned 


What you need to be successful:
• Valid Washington State Driver’s license and Insurance
• Ability to travel through Canada-US border 


Schedule: Monday through Friday from 7:30AM to 4:00PM 


Pay: $22.00 - $36.00 (DOE) 


Double the Reward Referral Program
Do you know someone seeking employment or exploring new career opportunities? Refer them to Express Employment Professionals in Mount Vernon. Once your referral completes 80 hours of work, you’ll both receive a $100 reward as a thank you. It’s a nice and easy way to support someone’s job search while earning a bonus for yourself. Refer. Reward. Repeat.


HOW TO APPLY:
- CALL OUR OFFICE at for an immediate phone interview 
- VISIT OUR OFFICE at 1806 Riverside Dr. Ste D, Mount Vernon, WA 98273 for an immediate in-person interview
- APPLY ONLINE at https://apply.expresspros.com
- EMAIL RESUME to jobs.mountvernonwa@expresspros.com


View our available jobs at https://www.expresspros.com/us-washington-mount-vernon

Download the Express Jobs App wherever you get your apps
Search through available jobs, apply, receive information about your assignments, track your hours, and more

About Express Employment Professionals: 
Express can help you find the type of job that is a perfect fit for your needs and abilities, and you'll never pay a fee for our services and support. Express can place you in a wide range of jobs that meet your employment goals, including Administrative, Commercial and Professional positions. Let us help you today! Come meet our staff! We would love to sit down with you to help you determine your next career opportunity! 

Benefits Available to Eligible Express Associates: 
- Medical Plan 
- Dental Plan 
- Holiday Pay (6 Days Observed) 
- 401(K) Retirement Savings Plan 
- Vision/Eyewear Plan 
- Life Insurance 
- Short-Term Disability 
- Referral Bonuses

We look forward to partnering with you today!

Description

TAK Broadband is a leading end-to-end U.S. fiber broadband network construction contractor operating in 42 states. It builds more than just networks; it connects communities to new valuable digital opportunities. TAK offers comprehensive service solutions, from construction to drops to fulfillment. TAK’s ecosystem of partners allows it to successfully complete every project starting from the first point of conception. This includes engineering teams, distributors, and more than 100 qualified construction crews with extensive experience across all ISP types and markets; over 300 experienced bury drop crews; and more than 700 professional technicians.




We are seeking a Construction Supervisor to join our team in Little Canada, MN. In this role you will support all phases of construction projects and manage all aspects of the day-to-day operations of team members. 




Why TAK?

Full TimePaid WeeklyCompensation: $65K - $80K annually, DOEFull Benefits Package (Medical, Dental & Vision)Paid Time Off401(k) with Company Match!25K Company Paid Life InsuranceIndependent Work & Team CollaborationCareer Development & Advancement Opportunities! The Role

Hire, train and develop talent for all roles Support all phases of construction projects Develop skillsets and knowledge of construction teams – both aerial and underground Manage payroll and overtime guidelines; manage productivity levelsComplete employee performance management functions; disciplinary actions, performance reviews and ongoing development Will be the team’s “expert” and “go to” resource; spending time in the field to understand, improve and build out the teamReview, update and maintain team dataManage all aspects of the day-to-day operations of team members; headcount requirementsWork with leaders to delegate tasks when relevant Keep safety as #1 priority for each team member: adhere to company safety standards and all federal, state and local lawsDrive team to meet deadlines and produce quality projectsProvide a high level of customer service when interfacing with customersGive direction to multiple crews and their job site activitiesBuild and maintain positive relationships with utilities, clients, permitting agencies and government officials as neededHandle customer and contractor problems related to projectsWork in a variety of environments; indoors, outdoors, elevated, tight spaces etc.Maintain open communications with other departmentsOther duties as assignedRequirements

3  years of aerial and/or underground telecommunications construction leadership experience requiredPrior construction industry experience required; coax splicing, fiber splicing, directional drilling, map reading, etc.Ability to be flexible and manage changing prioritiesAbility to travel in market and occasionally to other markets, open to a variety of schedules and accessible outside of normal business hours as neededExcellent customer service, time management, problem-solving and troubleshooting skillsAbility to communicate effectively in-person or virtuallyAbility to build and maintain positive relationships with internal and external customersAbility to give feedback; positive and negative when neededStrong desire to grow and develop team membersAbility to complete work indoors, outdoors, in tight spaces and elevated by bending, reaching, twisting, climbing as neededAbility to safely navigate various terrains, managing equipment and toolsA body weight of no more than 275 pounds to perform ladder work safely. **Safety is our #1 Priority. If personal safety is not compromised, an accommodation may be available based on previous ladder experience in a similar role**Must provide a valid government-issued photo ID for verification; a driver’s license is required if the role involves driving; Class A CDL a plusPassing of all pre-employment requirements (MVR, Background Check, Drug Screen)


The compensation and benefits information is accurate as of the date of this posting. The Company reserves the right to modify this information at any time, with or without notice, subject to applicable law.




TAK is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, genetic information, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. titre: Project Supervisor, Telecom Construction in Little Canada
description:
Energy Jobline is the largest and fastest growing global Energy Job Board and Energy Hub. We have an audience reach of over 7 million energy professionals, 400,000+ monthly advertised global energy and engineering jobs, and work with the leading energy companies worldwide.


We focus on the Oil & Gas, Renewables, Engineering, Power, and Nuclear markets as well as emerging technologies in EV, Battery, and Fusion. We are committed to ensuring that we offer the most exciting career opportunities from around the world for our jobseekers.


Job DescriptionJob DescriptionDescription:


TAK Broadband is a leading end-to-end U.S. fiber broadband network construction contractor operating in 42 states. It builds more than just networks; it connects communities to new valuable digital opportunities. TAK offers comprehensive service solutions, from construction to drops to fulfillment. TAK’s ecosystem of partners allows it to successfully complete every project starting from the first point of conception. This includes engineering teams, distributors, and more than 100 qualified construction crews with extensive experience across all ISP types and markets; over 300 experienced bury drop crews; and more than 700 professional technicians.






We are seeking a Project Supervisor to join our team in Little Canada, MN. In this role you will be assisting the Construction Manager and team for the aerial and underground construction business. Responsibilities will include administrative functions, preparing reports, customer service and all other duties as needed by the department.






Why TAK?



Full Time
Paid Weekly
Compensation: $66K - $78K annually, DOE
Company provided vehicle, laptop, & phone
Full Benefits Package (Medical, Dental & Vision)
Paid Time Off
401(k) with Company Match!
25K Company Paid Life Insurance
Independent Work & Team Collaboration
Career Development & Advancement Opportunities!

The Role



Daily field visits to ensure projects are following safety, quality control and productivity standards
Drive projects through systems for final development and budgeting
Assists with defining labor and material needs, permitting requirements/needs, infrastructure nodes and plant connection points, locate services and city/county/state requirements and restrictions
Define projects from job definitions, network integration needs and connection points
Manage overall path planning review, including site pictures, node integration and area resources including buildings, ground, and aerial assets
Support Quality Control functions; onsite quality control checks, job hazard analysis, safety audits etc.
Participate in the development of “Project Packets”
Project development for new proposed projects
Manage multiple stages of projects, keeping systems and records updated
Network and asset knowledge and planning to include aerial and underground assets and network inventory knowledge
Provide excellent customer experience every day
Other duties as assigned

Requirements:



3+ years of telecommunications construction industry experience required, aerial and underground (General construction, utility locating, oil field construction experience or other unrelated fields are not considered)
Previous leadership experience a plus
Ability to travel 20-25% of the time and open to a variety of schedules; evenings and/or weekends as needed
Ability to read and understand maps, drawings, and diagrams for project build process
Excellent customer service, time management, problem-solving and troubleshooting skills
Ability to learn and utilize software systems
Ability to communicate effectively with internal and external customers
Ability to travel, open to a variety of schedules; nights and/or weekends as needed
Ability to lift and carry up to 50lbs as needed
Ability to complete work outdoors, in tight spaces and elevated by bending, reaching, twisting, climbing, and working while standing
Ability to safely work and navigate various terrains and job sites
Must provide a valid government-issued photo ID for verification; a driver’s license is required if the role involves driving
Class A CDL (commercial driver's license) 
Passing of all pre-employment requirements (MVR, Background Check, Drug Screen)





The compensation and benefits information is accurate as of the date of this posting. The Company reserves the right to modify this information at any time, with or without notice, subject to applicable law.

We maintain full compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act and are committed to providing equal access throughout our employment practices. Individuals with disabilities may request reasonable accommodations during the application and hiring process by contacting TAKRecruiter@takbroadband.com.

titre: Technicien·ne Support IT - Genève ou Vevey
description:
À proposLe Groupe ANSAM, créé en 2020, est un leader des services informatiques en Suisse romande. Avec 5 bureaux et plus de 200 collaborateurs·rices, ANSAM travaille à créer une valeur ajoutée forte pour nos clients en répondant à l’ensemble de leurs besoins IT : conseil, projets, cybersécurité, opérations, recrutement, et intelligence artificielle.



💥 Support IT à Genève ou Vevey – Ici, chaque journée compte ! 💥

Marre des jobs où tu es juste un numéro ? Ici, chaque ticket, chaque incident, chaque client compte. Tu interviens, tu résous, tu brilles.

 

🛠️Ce que tu feras :

Résoudre les incidents directement chez nos clients, comme un pro sur le terrain.

Gérer les tickets et la boîte mail partagée avec rapidité et efficacité.

Documenter tes solutions pour que toute l’équipe avance plus vite.

Appliquer ITIL, parler français et anglais (niveau C1), et même allemand si tu maîtrises cet item.



📱Ce qu’on cherche :

Titulaire d’un CFC ou équivalent IT

Expérience multiclient obligatoire – tu sais jongler avec plusieurs environnements et clients sans perdre le sourire.

Permis de conduire et casier vierge

💻 Ton arsenal IT :

Postes de travail et clients légers (IGEL, ThinClient) – tes outils pour dompter les systèmes

Microsoft & O365 – tes gadgets pour gérer tous les environnements

Virtualisation : ESXi, VSphere, Virtual Center, Citrix, MS Terminal Server – ton laboratoire de tests et d’expériences

Active Directory & Azure AD – tes boucliers pour sécuriser les accès et comptes

Gestion des devices utilisateurs·trices (tablettes, smartphones) – toujours prêt·e à intervenir partout

Connaissance de 3CX ? Ton canal secret pour communiquer efficacement avec l’équipe



🔥Ce qu’on offre :

Horaires 7h–19h en rotation, télétravail possible après la période d’essai

Une équipe soudée et fun

Animaux domestiques autorisés : ton chien ou ton chat peut te rejoindre au bureau

Missions variées : jamais deux journées identiques

Café à volonté et bonne humeur garanties

🚀 Ici, tu ne subis pas ton job : tu le conduis.
📩 Postule maintenant et rejoins notre équipe IT à Genève ou Vevey


Notre visionChez ANSAM, nous plaçons l’humain, l’entrepreneuriat et la durabilité au cœur de notre culture d’entreprise.

Notre mission : accompagner les entreprises romandes dans leur digitalisation à travers une informatique à valeur ajoutée innovante, durable et responsable.

Le mot ANSAM, qui signifie ensemble, reflète notre manière de travailler : en co-création, avec nos équipes comme avec nos clients.

Nos modes de collaboration sont adaptés aux besoins de notre époque :

Postes adaptables entre 80 % et 100 %,

Jusqu’à 20 jours de congés sans solde (KTB),

Télétravail possible jusqu’à 40 % du temps,

Amenez vos animaux de compagnie au bureau

Chaque collaborateur·rice bénéficie de formations continues, d’un environnement moderne et ergonomique dans nos cinq bureaux autour de l’arc lémanique (Genève, Lausanne, Nyon, Vevey, Sion), et de réelles perspectives d’évolution au sein du groupe.

Nos valeursChez ANSAM, nos valeurs guident nos relations et notre manière de collaborer au quotidien. Ainsi, si vous nous rejoignez, c’est que vous partagez ces valeurs :

Entrepreneuriat : Être créatif·ve et ambitieux·euse, innover, évoluer, apprendre en continu, prendre et assumer ses responsabilités, savoir se remettre en question et s’engager pleinement pour l’entreprise.

Solidarité : Être attentif·ve aux autres, aider, faire vivre l’esprit d’équipe et communiquer ouvertement pour créer un environnement harmonieux, solidaire et bienveillant.

Intégrité : Agir avec éthique et honnêteté, faire preuve de transparence, respecter les autres et la diversité, et appliquer le principe d’équité pour construire un climat de confiance.

Qualité : Avoir le sens du service, être compétent·e et fiable, tenir ses engagements et viser l’excellence pour garantir un haut niveau de satisfaction et de performance.

    Expérience multiclient obligatoire – tu sais jongler avec plusieurs environnements et clients sans perdre le sourire.


  
   Permis de conduire et casier vierge


 
  Ton arsenal IT :


 
  
   Postes de travail et clients légers (IGEL, ThinClient) – tes outils pour dompter les systèmes


  
   Microsoft & O365 – tes gadgets pour gérer tous les environnements


  
   Virtualisation : ESXi, VSphere, Virtual Center, Citrix, MS Terminal Server – ton laboratoire de tests et d’expériences


  
   Active Directory & Azure AD – tes boucliers pour sécuriser les accès et comptes


  
   Gestion des devices utilisateurs·trices (tablettes, smartphones) – toujours prêt·e à intervenir partout


  
   Connaissance de 3CX ? Ton canal secret pour communiquer efficacement avec l’équipe


 
 


  Ce qu’on offre :


 
  
   Horaires 7h–19h en rotation, télétravail possible après la période d’essai


  
   Une équipe soudée et fun


  
   Animaux domestiques autorisés : ton chien ou ton chat peut te rejoindre au bureau


  
   Missions variées : jamais deux journées identiques


  
   Café à volonté et bonne humeur garanties


 
  Ici, tu ne subis pas ton job : tu le conduis.

   Postule maintenant et rejoins notre équipe IT à Genève ou Vevey


 

    Notre vision
 Chez ANSAM, nous plaçons l’humain, l’entrepreneuriat et la durabilité au cœur de notre culture d’entreprise.


 Notre mission : accompagner les entreprises romandes dans leur digitalisation à travers une informatique à valeur ajoutée innovante, durable et responsable.


 Le mot ANSAM, qui signifie ensemble, reflète notre manière de travailler : en co-création, avec nos équipes comme avec nos clients.


 Nos modes de collaboration sont adaptés aux besoins de notre époque :


 
  
   Postes adaptables entre 80 % et 100 %,


  
   Jusqu’à 20 jours de congés sans solde (KTB),


  
    Télétravail possible jusqu’à 40 % du temps,


  
   Amenez vos animaux de compagnie au bureau


 
 Chaque collaborateur·rice bénéficie de formations continues, d’un environnement moderne et ergonomique dans nos cinq bureaux autour de l’arc lémanique (Genève, Lausanne, Nyon, Vevey, Sion), et de réelles perspectives d’évolution au sein du groupe.


  Nos valeurs
 Chez ANSAM, nos valeurs guident nos relations et notre manière de collaborer au quotidien. Ainsi, si vous nous rejoignez, c’est que vous partagez ces valeurs :


 
  
    Entrepreneuriat : Être créatif·ve et ambitieux·euse, innover, évoluer, apprendre en continu, prendre et assumer ses responsabilités, savoir se remettre en question et s’engager pleinement pour l’entreprise.


  
    Solidarité : Être attentif·ve aux autres, aider, faire vivre l’esprit d’équipe et communiquer ouvertement pour créer un environnement harmonieux, solidaire et bienveillant.


  
    Intégrité : Agir avec éthique et honnêteté, faire preuve de transparence, respecter les autres et la diversité, et appliquer le principe d’équité pour construire un climat de confiance.


  
    Qualité : Avoir le sens du service, être compétent·e et fiable, tenir ses engagements et viser l’excellence pour garantir un haut niveau de satisfaction et de performance.

About The Role

Imagine stepping into a place where every system you touch powers teams that are redefining aerospace and defense. As our Systems and Networks Administrator, you will play a key role in managing, securing, and optimizing a broad range of IT systems across the organization. Acting as a versatile “jack of all trades,” this role requires deep experience across core platforms such as Microsoft 365, AWS, Jira, Windows Server, and network technologies, while maintaining a holistic and proactive security mindset. We are looking for someone who can ensure the reliability, scalability, and security of all systems and infrastructure supporting business operations.

At Destinus, we are revolutionizing the defense industry with cutting edge Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs). Our innovative technologies are designed to meet the unique demands of modern defense operations, delivering unparalleled speed, precision, and cost effectiveness. Destinus partners with government agencies and defense organizations worldwide to provide advanced solutions for mission critical operations, enabling a new era of efficiency and technological superiority. Join us in shaping the future of defense with groundbreaking aerospace innovations.



What You´ll Do

Administration, monitoring and optimization of Microsoft 365 services (Exchange Online, SharePoint, Teams, Defender, Entra ID, Intune).Manage and maintain cloud infrastructure in AWS, including identity sources, networking, security controls, HPC, SAML, Route53.Support and maintain Windows Server environments: Active Directory, file services, system patching, back-ups.Administer and customize Atlassian Jira and Confluence, ensuring alignment with team workflows and integrations.Collaborate in network infrastructure operations with switches, routers, firewalls, VPNs, site connectivity, and basic SD-WAN operations. Design of networks and subnet allocation for new facilities and maintenance, monitoring, and incident resolution.Apply security best practices across systems and networks, ensuring compliance, monitoring, and incident response readiness.Troubleshoot complex infrastructure issues across systems, networks, cloud platforms, and applications.Consultations of IT support specialists regarding software and hardware settings, assistance in resolving complex incidents.Contribute to IT strategy discussions and propose ways of implementation, improvements to enhance performance, security, and reliability of IT systems.Maintain documentation of IT systems, configurations, and processes.Requirements

What You’ll Need

Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or equivalent experience.5+ years in systems and network administration, with broad cross-platform exposure.Certifications such as Microsoft 365 Administrator, AWS Associate/Professional, Network+, CCNA, or security-focused credentials (e.g., Security+) are advantageous.Strong Microsoft 365 administration skills, including identity, access control, automation, device management, monitoring, and incident resolution.Knowledge of Zero Trust principles and modern cybersecurity frameworks. ISO27001, ABDO, NIS, etc.Familiarity with monitoring platforms (PRTG, SolarWinds, CloudWatch, SIEM tools, SOC/NOC).Linux administration (basic to intermediate).Exposure to DevOps practices and CI/CD tooling.Understanding virtualization solutions (VMware/Hyper-V) and storage technologies.Strong Windows Server and Active Directory skills.Competent with AWS services, security groups, IAM, networking, and resource configuration.Deep understanding of OIDC, SAML 2.0, SCIM, and MFA flows. Experience integrating complex SaaS platforms (e.g., Salesforce, Slack, HRIS tools) with Entra ID.Experience with automation or scripting (PowerShell, Python, Bash).Solid understanding of network fundamentals (TCP/IP, VLANs, routing, firewalls, VPNs).Strong Windows Server and Active Directory skills.Ability to translate technical constraints into business solutions for non-technical stakeholders.

Who You Are

You move fast, think sharp, and stay steady when things get complex. You love diving into mixed tech environments and solving problems across systems without getting boxed in. Ownership motivates you, collaboration fuels you, and security is always on your mind. You communicate clearly, act with integrity, and bring a proactive, improvement-driven mindset to everything you touch. You want to make real impact in a place where innovation is the everyday baseline.

 

Benefits

Why Join Us?

✨ The Thrill of the Future – Work on cutting-edge projects that redefine aerospace.
 ✨ Global Innovation Hub – Collaborate with top engineers from all over the world.
 ✨ Fast-Paced & High-Growth – Experience the rush of working in a rapidly expanding company.
 ✨ Real Impact – Your work won’t be theoretical—it will shape the future of flight.

Destinus is an equal-opportunity employer. We value diversity in our companies and our teams and do not discriminate based on age, race, religion, color, country of origin, gender, sexual orientation, or disability status.

 Destinus – Challenge the limits of time, space, and human potential.

Kein Tag gleicht dem anderen. Du bist Ansprechperson, Troubleshooter und Systemhüter in einem - für Kunden mit Cloud-Setup genauso wie für solche mit gewachsener On-Prem-Infrastruktur. Störungen be-hebst du per Fernwartung oder direkt vor Ort, komplexe Second-Level-Fälle gehst du strukturiert an, und die ICT-Services deiner Kunden behältst du jederzeit im Blick. Je nach Profil ist auch eine Doppel-rolle als System- und Netzwerk Administrator denkbar - für alle, die gerne etwas breiter aufgestellt sind. Was du mitbringst Du bist eine empathische Persönlichkeit und arbeitest engagiert sowie selbständig.

Sehr gute Kenntnisse im Microsoft 365 Cloud-Umfeld (AVD, Exchange Online, Entra, Intune, Teams, etc.)
Solides Wissen in Netzwerktechnik (TCP/IP, Routing, Switches, Firewall-Grundlagen)
Hohe Dienstleistungsbereitschaft und Kommunikationsfähigkeit
Stilsicheres Deutsch und verhandlungssicheres Englisch
Erfahrungen mit Firewall-Lösungen wie Sophos
Kenntnisse im Bereich Backup- und Recovery-Lösungen
Erste Erfahrungen als Netzwerk Administrator oder Interesse an einer Doppelrolle

titre: Technicien·ne Support IT - Genève ou Vevey
description:
À propos
 Le Groupe ANSAM, créé en 2020, est un leader des services informatiques en Suisse romande. Avec 5 bureaux et plus de 200 collaborateurs·rices, ANSAM travaille à créer une valeur ajoutée forte pour nos clients en répondant à l’ensemble de leurs besoins IT : conseil, projets, cybersécurité, opérations, recrutement, et intelligence artificielle.


 

    Support IT à Genève ou Vevey – Ici, chaque journée compte ! 


 Marre des jobs où tu es juste un numéro ? Ici, chaque ticket, chaque incident, chaque client compte. Tu interviens, tu résous, tu brilles.


 


 

    ️Ce que tu feras :


 
  
   Résoudre les incidents directement chez nos clients, comme un pro sur le terrain.


  
   Gérer les tickets et la boîte mail partagée avec rapidité et efficacité.


  
   Documenter tes solutions pour que toute l’équipe avance plus vite.


  
   Appliquer ITIL, parler français et anglais (niveau C1), et même allemand si tu maîtrises cet item.


 
 


  Ce qu’on cherche :


 
  
   Titulaire d’un CFC ou équivalent IT


  
    Expérience multiclient obligatoire – tu sais jongler avec plusieurs environnements et clients sans perdre le sourire.


  
   Permis de conduire et casier vierge


 
  Ton arsenal IT :


 
  
   Postes de travail et clients légers (IGEL, ThinClient) – tes outils pour dompter les systèmes


  
   Microsoft & O365 – tes gadgets pour gérer tous les environnements


  
   Virtualisation : ESXi, VSphere, Virtual Center, Citrix, MS Terminal Server – ton laboratoire de tests et d’expériences


  
   Active Directory & Azure AD – tes boucliers pour sécuriser les accès et comptes


  
   Gestion des devices utilisateurs·trices (tablettes, smartphones) – toujours prêt·e à intervenir partout


  
   Connaissance de 3CX ? Ton canal secret pour communiquer efficacement avec l’équipe


 
 


  Ce qu’on offre :


 
  
   Horaires 7h–19h en rotation, télétravail possible après la période d’essai


  
   Une équipe soudée et fun


  
   Animaux domestiques autorisés : ton chien ou ton chat peut te rejoindre au bureau


  
   Missions variées : jamais deux journées identiques


  
   Café à volonté et bonne humeur garanties


 
  Ici, tu ne subis pas ton job : tu le conduis.

   Postule maintenant et rejoins notre équipe IT à Genève ou Vevey


 

    Notre vision
 Chez ANSAM, nous plaçons l’humain, l’entrepreneuriat et la durabilité au cœur de notre culture d’entreprise.


 Notre mission : accompagner les entreprises romandes dans leur digitalisation à travers une informatique à valeur ajoutée innovante, durable et responsable.


 Le mot ANSAM, qui signifie ensemble, reflète notre manière de travailler : en co-création, avec nos équipes comme avec nos clients.


 Nos modes de collaboration sont adaptés aux besoins de notre époque :


 
  
   Postes adaptables entre 80 % et 100 %,


  
   Jusqu’à 20 jours de congés sans solde (KTB),


  
    Télétravail possible jusqu’à 40 % du temps,


  
   Amenez vos animaux de compagnie au bureau


 
 Chaque collaborateur·rice bénéficie de formations continues, d’un environnement moderne et ergonomique dans nos cinq bureaux autour de l’arc lémanique (Genève, Lausanne, Nyon, Vevey, Sion), et de réelles perspectives d’évolution au sein du groupe.


  Nos valeurs
 Chez ANSAM, nos valeurs guident nos relations et notre manière de collaborer au quotidien. Ainsi, si vous nous rejoignez, c’est que vous partagez ces valeurs :


 
  
    Entrepreneuriat : Être créatif·ve et ambitieux·euse, innover, évoluer, apprendre en continu, prendre et assumer ses responsabilités, savoir se remettre en question et s’engager pleinement pour l’entreprise.


  
    Solidarité : Être attentif·ve aux autres, aider, faire vivre l’esprit d’équipe et communiquer ouvertement pour créer un environnement harmonieux, solidaire et bienveillant.


  
    Intégrité : Agir avec éthique et honnêteté, faire preuve de transparence, respecter les autres et la diversité, et appliquer le principe d’équité pour construire un climat de confiance.


  
    Qualité : Avoir le sens du service, être compétent·e et fiable, tenir ses engagements et viser l’excellence pour garantir un haut niveau de satisfaction et de performance.

Le département Ressources humaines des sociétés du groupe Ofac recrute :

description: Une institution médiatique majeure en Suisse romande recherche un(e) Technicien(ne) Informatique Senior pour rejoindre son équipe de support interne. En tant que société de services informatiques suisse romande, nous apportons expérience et savoir-faire aux organisations privées et publiques. Dans le cadre de notre développement et pour renforcer notre équipe, nous sommes à la recherche d’un(e) :

titre: Spécialiste IT et Innovateur Support (h/f/d) (80-100%) - Zurich
description:
Rejoignez SMG Swiss Marketplace Group, un leader digital en Suisse. Contribuez à un environnement de travail innovant et dynamique.


Tâches

Assurer un support technique efficace pour 800+ collaborateurs.
Analyser et résoudre des défis techniques avec rapidité.
Développer et maintenir la documentation technique pour l'équipe.

Compétences

Passion pour l'assistance technique et compétences en communication.
Connaissance des applications modernes comme Google Workspace.
Esprit d'équipe et capacité à travailler de manière autonome.

Description de l'entrepriseBienvenue chez SMG Swiss Marketplace Group AG

Nous sommes un réseau de places de marché en ligne en Suisse et une entreprise numérique européenne de premier plan. Avec nos produits innovants, nous simplifions la vie quotidienne de tous nos clients.

 

Description du posteRejoignez notre équipe de support et façonnez le paysage numérique de la Suisse.



Faites notre connaissance !

Andreas Wüest - Head of Workplace & Service Desk - LinkedIn





Votre mission

En tant que membre de notre équipe Workplace & Service Desk, vous êtes le premier point de contact pour plus de 800 collaborateurs et vous assurez que leurs demandes techniques sont résolues rapidement et efficacement. Grâce à votre engagement, vous garantissez le bon fonctionnement de nos équipes tout en promouvant des solutions innovantes qui améliorent nos espaces de travail numériques.

 

Ce qui vous attend

Support avec passion : Fournir un support compétent sur site et à distance pour les questions liées au matériel, aux logiciels, à la gestion des comptes et à la connectivitéTrouver des solutions : Analyser les défis techniques, effectuer des diagnostics et développer des solutions aidant nos collaborateursPartager les connaissances : Créer et maintenir des documentations techniques pour faire progresser toute l'équipeVivre l'innovation : Identifier de manière proactive les potentiels d'optimisation et mettre en œuvre des solutions intelligentes et durablesTravail de projet : Prendre en charge de manière autonome de petits projets de l'idée à la réalisationRéseautage : Se connecter avec différentes équipes et départements pour créer des solutions globalesAvoir un impact : Votre soutien permet à nos équipes de donner le meilleur d'elles-mêmes 

QualificationsCe que vous apportez

Passion pour un support excellent et envie d'aider les autresExcellentes compétences en communication en allemand et anglais sont indispensablesCapacités de résolution de problèmes et sens du détailPensée stratégique et innovante avec un focus sur la vision globaleInitiative personnelle et capacité à mener des projets de manière autonomeExpérience dans la documentation des processus et solutions techniquesEsprit d'équipe avec de fortes compétences en réseautageConnaissances techniques solides dans des applications modernes telles que Google Workspace, Workspace ONE MDM, Okta, Slack, Notion, Miro, etc.

 Informations supplémentairesCe que nous vous offrons 🤝

6 semaines de vacances (avec possibilité d'acheter jusqu'à 10 jours supplémentaires)Semaine de travail de 40 heures18 semaines de congé maternité et 6 semaines de congé paternité. Valable également en cas d'adoption.Modèles de travail flexibles (l'équilibre vie professionnelle/vie privée est très important pour nous)Accès à des plateformes pour la santé mentale, le bien-être et le conseilAbonnement demi-tarif pour les transports publics et voyages en 1ère classe en train entre les sites SMG en Suisse

Processus de recrutement 📆

Pour ce poste, vous devez déjà résider en Suisse et disposer d'une autorisation de travail valide ou de la citoyenneté suisse.

Le recrutement est pour nous un échange sur un pied d'égalité. Nous souhaitons vous connaître et vous donner la chance de nous découvrir. Voici à quoi vous attendre :

Premier entretien de prise de contact avec un recruteur (30 min)Entretien avec le responsable du recrutement (60 min)Entretien sur site avec étude de cas et possibilité de rencontrer l'équipe (150 min)



 Prêt à faire la différence ?

Si vous aimez aider les autres avec passion tout en contribuant à façonner l'avenir de notre espace de travail numérique, nous sommes impatients de vous rencontrer.



Postulez dès maintenant avec votre CV actuel ainsi que vos certificats de travail et rejoignez Swiss Marketplace Group AG.





 

La diversité chez SMG

Chez SMG, nous valorisons les perspectives diverses et croyons au potentiel de chacun. Nous accueillons les candidatures de personnes aux parcours variés, même si vous ne remplissez pas toutes les exigences. Ensemble, nous développons des compétences, valorisons les différences et créons de l'impact.





 

#LI-Hybrid









*Note pour les agences

Swiss Marketplace Group AG travaille uniquement directement avec les candidats et n'accepte pas les CV envoyés spontanément par des agences de recrutement, sauf si ceux-ci ont été expressément demandés au préalable. Les soumissions sans autorisation préalable ne seront ni prises en compte ni répondues. Nous apprécions la compréhension et la coopération des agences pour respecter cette politique.





 

À propos de nous



SMG Swiss Marketplace Group AG est un réseau innovant de places de marché en ligne et une entreprise numérique innovante qui simplifie la vie des gens avec des produits tournés vers l'avenir.



SMG Swiss Marketplace Group AG offre aux clients les meilleurs outils pour les accompagner dans leurs décisions importantes de vie. Le portefeuille comprend l'immobilier (ImmoScout24, Homegate, Flatfox, alle-immobilien.ch, Immostreet.ch, home.ch, Publimmo, Acheter-Louer.ch, CASASOFT, IAZI), l'automobile (AutoScout24, MotoScout24), les places de marché générales (anibis.ch, tutti.ch, Ricardo) ainsi que les finances & assurances (FinanceScout24, moneyland.ch).







Clause de non-responsabilité / Note pour les agences

SMG Swiss Marketplace Group AG travaille principalement directement avec les candidats. Nous n'acceptons pas les CV ou profils de candidats envoyés spontanément par des agences de recrutement, sauf si nous avons expressément demandé leur soutien pour certains postes.

Toutes les soumissions sans demande préalable de notre équipe Talent Acquisition ne seront ni prises en compte ni confirmées.

Nous remercions les agences de recrutement pour leur compréhension et leur coopération dans le respect de cette politique.
